phase_note: Phase 1 anchors import behavior around native root-module member chains, canonical import resolution, cache shape, and duplicate-import diagnostics.

cases.upstream_focus: Import fixtures should be organized as small reusable module graphs so cyclic and layered cases stay readable.
cases.zr_decision: ZR reference fixtures will keep import scenarios as small named graphs rather than one monolithic module test file.

cases.upstream_focus: Runtime import execution needs explicit coverage for cyclic graphs and cached module identity.
cases.zr_decision: ZR import testing should distinguish parser acceptance from runtime import graph behavior and cache reuse.

cases.upstream_focus: Import tests need to cover cache behavior, loader interactions, and repeated import semantics instead of only first-load success.
cases.zr_decision: ZR import manifests track cache-sensitive cases so repeated `%import(...)` behavior can be asserted later.

cases.upstream_focus: Imported member visibility must remain resolvable through layered scope construction, not only through direct module handles.
cases.zr_decision: ZR should validate root-module member chains such as `%import("zr.system").vm.state()` as a first-class import path.

cases.upstream_focus: Canonical names and import-path normalization must be tested explicitly to avoid ambiguous resolution.
cases.zr_decision: ZR import manifests record canonical-path expectations before expanding cross-project import fixtures.

cases.upstream_focus: Duplicate imports should have deterministic scope behavior and diagnostics instead of silently diverging by load order.
cases.zr_decision: ZR needs duplicate-import and repeated-load regression cases in later phases; phase 1 records the precedent now.
